RNA fragments were dephosphorylated and ligated to a pre-adenylated 3’ DNA adaptor (5’-rAppTNNNTAGNNNTGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGG-NH2-3’) with T4 RNA Ligase 2, truncated KQ (NEB, M0373) at 25°C for 2.5 hours and then at 22°C for at least 2.5 hours. RNA was purified with an RNA clean & concentration kit (Zymo Research; R1013). RNA was annealed with a reverse transcription primer (5’-GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA-3’) at 75°C for 5 min, 37°C for 15 min and 25°C for 15 min. Reverse transcription was performed using SuperScript III (Invitrogen) in the presence of 5 mM Mg2+ at 55°C for 30 min and at 50°C for 30 min. RNA was hydrolyzed by adding 1.5 μL of 1 M NaOH and incubation at 75°C for 15 min. cDNA was purified by 8% urea PAGE to remove free primers and adaptors. The 3’ DNA adaptors (5’-PO4-GNNNATNNNAGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGT-NH2-3’ and 5’-PO4-ANNNCGNNNAGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGT-NH2-3’, where N is a random sequence) were ligated to cDNA with T4 RNA Ligase 1 (NEB; M0204) in the presence of ATP at 25°C overnight. DNA was extracted with an equal volume of phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) and precipitated by ethanol. DNA library was PCR-amplified with Phusion polymerase (NEB; M0530) and indexed primers P5 and P7, purified on 5% native TBE gel by crush and soak and sequenced on Illumina Hiseq X10.

RNA fragments underwent dephosphorylation prior to being ligated to a pre-adenylated 3’ DNA adaptor using T4 RNA Ligase 2, truncated KQ for 2.5 hours at 25°C followed by 2.5 hours at 22°C. RNA was then purified using an RNA clean & concentration kit (Zymo Research; R1013) and annealed to a reverse transcription primer (5’-GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA-3’) at 75°C for 5 minutes followed by 37°C for 15 minutes and 25°C for 15 minutes. Reverse transcription was performed using SuperScript III (Invitrogen) with 5 mM Mg2+ for 30 minutes at 55°C followed by an additional 30 minutes at 50°C. RNA was hydrolyzed by adding 1.5 μL of 1 M NaOH and incubation at 75°C for 15 minutes. cDNA was purified using 8% urea PAGE to remove free primers and adaptors. The cDNA was then ligated using T4 RNA Ligase 1 (NEB; M0204) in the presence of ATP with 3’ DNA adaptors (5’-PO4-GNNNATNNNAGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGT-NH2-3’ and 5’-PO4-ANNNCGNNNAGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGT-NH2-3’, where N is a random sequence) overnight at 25°C. DNA was extracted with an equal volume of phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) and precipitated by ethanol. DNA library was finally PCR-amplified with Phusion polymerase (NEB; M0530) and indexed primers P5 and P7, purified on 5% native TBE gel by crush and soak and sequenced using Illumina Hiseq X10 technology.

The cDNA was PCR-amplified using Phusion polymerase (NEB; M0530) with primers (5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACNNNNNACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-3' and 5'-CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATNNNNNNGTGACTGGAGTTCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA-3'), purified on 5% TBE gel and PCR product was sequenced on Illumina Hiseq X10.

The cDNA was amplified by PCR using Phusion polymerase (NEB; M0530) and two primers (5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACNNNNNACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-3' and 5'-CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATNNNNNNGTGACTGGAGTTCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA-3'). The amplified product was then purified on 5% TBE gel and finally sequenced on an Illumina Hiseq X10.

Adaptor sequences were trimmed using Flexbar with the options of error rate=0.2 and minimum length=33. PCR duplicates were removed by pyCRAC. Trimmed reads were mapped to the genome sequence of Sulfolobus islandicus REY15A with Hisat2 using no spliced alignment. SAMtools and bedtools were used subsequent analysis.
The 5' end of read 1 that corresponds to the 3' end of cDNA is counted, as well as the read coverage. Ψ-ratio is calculated for each position as 5' end counts/read coverage at +1 position. Ψ-fc is amount to log10(Ψ-ratio of CMC-treated RNA/ Ψ-ratio of untreated RNA).

The adaptor sequences were removed by using Flexbar with the options of an error rate of 0.2 and a minimum length of 33. Duplicate PCR reads were eliminated by pyCRAC. The trimmed reads were then aligned to the genome sequence of Sulfolobus islandicus REY15A using Hisat2 without any spliced alignment. Subsequent analysis was done using SAMtools and bedtools. The 5' end of read 1, which matches the 3' end of the cDNA, and the read coverage were counted. For each position, the Ψ-ratio was determined as 5' end counts divided by the read coverage at +1 position. The Ψ-fc was calculated as log10(Ψ-ratio of CMC-treated RNA/ Ψ-ratio of untreated RNA).

H/ACA RNP proteins from P. furiosus were expressed and purified as previously described (Li and Ye, 2006). The full-length genes of S. islandicus Cbf5, Nop10, Gar1 and L7Ae were PCR-amplified from the S. islandicus (REY15A) genomic DNA. The Cbf5 gene contains an intron and its two exons were separately amplified and joined by overlap PCR. L7Ae with a C-terminal His6 tag and Gar1 were individually cloned into multiple clone site 1 (MSC1) of pRSFDuet-1 plasmid. Cbf5-His6 and Nop10 were cloned into MCS1 and MSC2 of pETDuet-1, respectively, and co-expressed. All plasmids were transformed into Escherichia coli Rosetta (DE3) strains.

H/ACA RNP proteins from P. furiosus were purified as previously described by Li and Ye in 2006. The full-length genes of S. islandicus Cbf5, Nop10, Gar1, and L7Ae were PCR amplified from the S. islandicus (REY15A) genomic DNA. The Cbf5 gene has an intron, and its two exons were amplified separately and then joined by overlap PCR. L7Ae, which has a C-terminal His6 tag, and Gar1 were cloned individually into multiple clone site 1 (MSC1) of the pRSFDuet-1 plasmid. Cbf5-His6 and Nop10 were cloned into MSC1 and MSC2 of the pETDuet-1 plasmid, respectively, and co-expressed. All plasmids were then transformed into Escherichia coli Rosetta (DE3) strains.

L7Ae with a His-tag at its C-terminus was cloned into the multiple cloning site 1 (MSC1) of the pRSFDuet-1 plasmid. Gar1 were individually cloned into multiple clone site 1 (MSC1) of pRSFDuet-1 plasmid.

L7Ae, which has a His-tag at its C-terminus, was cloned into the multiple cloning site 1 (MSC1) of the pRSFDuet-1 plasmid. Similarly, Gar1 was also separately cloned into the multiple clone site 1 (MSC1) of the pRSFDuet-1 plasmid.

Cells co-expressing Cbf5-His6/Nop10 were harvested, resuspended in buffer A containing 1M NaCl, 25 mM phosphate (pH 7.6) and 5% glycerol and lysed by sonication. The lysate was heated at 76°C for 15 min and clarified by centrifugation. The supernatant was loaded onto a HisTrap column, which was washed with 20 mM imidazole in buffer A and eluted with 500 mM imidazole in buffer A. After 2-fold dilution, the proteins were loaded onto a Heparin column equilibrated in buffer containing 0.25 M NaCl and 25 mM phosphate (pH 7.6) and eluted at ~0.8 M NaCl in a 0.5-1 M salt gradient.

Cells that co-expressed Cbf5-His6/Nop10 were harvested, and resuspended in buffer A containing 1M NaCl, 25 mM phosphate (pH 7.6), and 5% glycerol. They were then lysed by sonication and heated at 76°C for 15 minutes before being clarified through centrifugation. The resulting supernatant was subjected to purification using a HisTrap column that was washed with buffer A containing 20 mM imidazole and ultimately eluted using buffer A with 500 mM imidazole. After a 2-fold dilution, the proteins underwent additional purification via a Heparin column equilibrated in a buffer that contained 0.25 M NaCl and 25 mM phosphate (pH 7.6). The proteins were then finally eluted with a 0.5-1 M salt gradient at ~0.8 M NaCl.
Gar1 was lysed in buffer A and heated as described above. After 2-fold dilution, Gar1 was loaded onto a Heparin column and eluted at ~0.5 M NaCl.
Gar1 was lysed in buffer A and heated, as described above. After a 2-fold dilution, Gar1 was subjected to purification through a Heparin column and was eluted using a ~0.5 M NaCl salt gradient.

Cell expressing L7Ae-His6 was lysed in buffer C containing 1M NaCl, 25 mM phosphate (pH 7.0) and 5% glycerol. Following heat treatment and clarification, L7Ae-His6 was loaded onto a HisTrap column, which was washed with 20 mM imidazole in buffer C and eluted with buffer M (500 mM imidazole and 20 mM MES, pH 6.0). L7Ae was loaded onto two serial HiTrap Blue HP columns in 20 mM MES, pH 6.0 and eluted at around 0.5 M NaCl in a salt gradient.

Cells that expressed L7Ae-His6 were lysed using buffer C, which contained 1M NaCl, 25 mM phosphate (pH 7.0), and 5% glycerol. Following heat treatment and clarification, L7Ae-His6 underwent purification using a HisTrap column which was first washed with buffer C that contained 20 mM imidazole. L7Ae-His6 was then eluted with buffer M, which contained 500 mM imidazole and 20 mM MES (pH 6.0). L7Ae was subsequently loaded onto two serial HiTrap Blue HP columns in 20 mM MES (pH 6.0) and ultimately eluted in a salt gradient at around 0.5 M NaCl.

The H/ACA RNA genes were amplified from the S. islandicus genomic DNA and inserted into the pBCSK(+) vector downstream of a T7 promoter. In vitro transcription reactions were performed at 37°C for 4 hours with T7 RNA polymerase and PCR products as templates. RNAs were resolved in an 8% polyacrylamide 8M-urea gel and purified. H/ACA RNPs were assembled from Cbf5-Nop10, L7Ae, Gar1 and H/ACA RNA with a molar ratio of 1:1:1:1:1.05 in buffer containing 20 mM HEPES (pH 8.2), 150 mM NaCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 1 mM DTT, 0.2 mM EDTA and 10% glycerol and incubated at 65°C for 3 min and then at 4°C.

The H/ACA RNA genes were amplified from the genomic DNA of S. islandicus, and were inserted downstream of a T7 promoter into the pBCSK(+) vector. In vitro transcription reactions were performed at 37°C for 4 hours using T7 RNA polymerase and PCR products as templates. RNAs were then resolved in an 8% polyacrylamide 8M-urea gel and subsequently purified. 

 H/ACA RNPs were assembled by combining Cbf5-Nop10, L7Ae, Gar1, and H/ACA RNA in a molar ratio of 1:1:1:1:1.05 in a buffer that contained 20 mM HEPES (pH 8.2), 150 mM NaCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 1 mM DTT, 0.2 mM EDTA, and 10% glycerol. The mixture was then incubated at 65°C for 3 minutes followed by a cooling step at 4°C.
